metadata.title: Kling API
metadata.description: Test and integrate Kling API for text-to-video, image-to-video, motion control, and AI avatar video generation.

title: Kling API
description: Kling is a video generation model family for creators and product teams that need flexible text-to-video and image-to-video workflows. This page brings the available Kling variants into one API-first experience so you can compare model behavior, pricing, request fields, and generated results.

Use Kling when your workflow needs cinematic motion, image-driven animation, motion control, or avatar-style video output. Choose a model version in the playground, test the output, then move the same model into your production API flow.
pricingNote: USD estimates use $0.005/credit with the best annual plan.

labels.apiDocsDetailDescription: Open the full API documentation for endpoints, authentication, task polling, and payload details.
labels.apiInputDescription: Request fields follow the selected Kling model, so the payload stays aligned with text, image, motion, or avatar generation.
labels.selectedModelNote: The playground, request sample, request fields, and pricing on this page follow the selected model type.

featuresTitle: What Kling API adds to your video workflow
featuresDescription: The page follows the same API-first structure as the reference: test the model, understand the fields, then move into integration.

capabilities.title: Text and image to video
capabilities.description: Generate clips from prompts or animate source images while keeping the selected Kling version, duration, and quality settings explicit.

capabilities.title: Cinematic motion
capabilities.description: Use Kling for dynamic camera movement, stylized action, product reveals, character shots, and short-form visual storytelling.

capabilities.title: Multiple model variants
capabilities.description: Switch between Kling 3.0, 2.6, 2.5 Turbo, 2.1, motion-control, and avatar variants without leaving the page.

capabilities.title: Reference-led output
capabilities.description: Use image or motion references where the selected model supports them, so generated clips can follow a clearer subject and scene direction.

quickStartTitle: From Kling test to production request
quickStartDescription: A compact integration path for teams that want to validate a Kling variant before wiring it into an app.

workflows.title: 1. Pick a Kling model
workflows.description: Choose the Kling version that matches your job, such as text-to-video, image-to-video, motion control, or avatar generation.

workflows.title: 2. Submit generation
workflows.description: Call the API with your account key, model id, prompt, media URLs, duration, resolution, and model-specific fields.

workflows.title: 3. Poll and deliver
workflows.description: Track the task until it succeeds, then retrieve the generated video URL for your app, campaign, or production pipeline.

useCasesTitle: What you can create with Kling API
useCasesDescription: Use the API when your product needs repeatable Kling video output, not just a one-off playground result.
useCases: Social clips for TikTok, Reels, and Shorts
Image animation for products, portraits, and concepts
Batch video production for templates and campaigns

communityShowcase.title: Successful Kling generations from the community
communityShowcase.description: Browse public Kling outputs that finished successfully, then open any case to inspect the prompt, settings, and result.

productionNotes.title: Account-gated execution
productionNotes.body: Users sign in, submit Kling jobs, and consume credits through the existing SdanceAI generation flow.

productionNotes.title: Media-aware requests
productionNotes.body: The studio supports uploaded reference media, direct URLs, previews, and form/API payload editing where the selected Kling model supports media input.

productionNotes.title: Version-specific control
productionNotes.body: Switch between Kling versions based on output type, quality target, price, and generation controls.

faqs.question: What is Kling API best for?
faqs.answer: It is best for short AI video generation workflows that need text-to-video, image-to-video, motion control, or avatar-style outputs.

faqs.question: Which Kling versions are available on this page?
faqs.answer: The page follows the configured Kling family in SdanceAI, including Kling 3.0, Kling 3.0 Motion Control, Kling 2.6, Kling 2.5 Turbo, Kling V2.1, and avatar variants.

faqs.question: Can I use an image reference with Kling API?
faqs.answer: Yes. Choose an image-to-video or media-capable Kling version, then provide the image URL or upload media through the studio flow.

faqs.question: Where can I test Kling before integrating?
faqs.answer: Use the playground on this page to test prompts, media inputs, model versions, request payloads, pricing, and output behavior before moving into production.

faqs.question: Does Kling API support motion control?
faqs.answer: Yes. The configured Kling family includes motion-control variants, and the request fields update based on the selected model.

faqs.question: Can I build avatar videos with Kling API?
faqs.answer: Yes. The configured Kling family includes AI avatar standard and pro variants for avatar-style video workflows.

faqs.question: How does pricing work?
faqs.answer: Pricing is shown per selected Kling model using the existing credit table. The page also shows an estimated USD cost based on the configured credit value.

faqs.question: Can I use Kling API for commercial projects?
faqs.answer: This page is configured for API workflows. For production use, review your account plan, credit balance, model availability, and any applicable content policy requirements.

cta.title: Turn Kling into your video workflow
cta.description: Open the generator, test a Kling version, then use the API docs when you are ready to wire Kling into production.
